c74object.name: fluid.knnclassifier~
c74object.description: A nearest neighbour classifier using a KDTree .

discussion.p: In order to make predictions, the KNNClassifier must first be fit with a fluid.dataset~ of data points and a target fluid.labelset~ with a label for each point in the DataSet (by means of a shared identifier).

To classify a point, numneighbours neighbours are determined for the incoming point and whichever class is most common among the neighbours is predicted as the class for the point. If an even number of numneighbours is requested and there is a tie, the label with the closer point will be predicted.
Read more about fluid.knnclassifier~ on the learn platform.
fluid.knnclassifier~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

description.p: Name for this instance, so that it can be reused or referred to by other data objects. If none is given, a name will be generated

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

method.name: refer
method.description: Changes the reference to the data in another named fluid.knnclassifier~ object. Changes to the data stored in any referenced will be shared by all other similarly named fluid.knnclassifier~ objects.

description.p: Fit the model to a source fluid.dataset~ and a target fluid.labelset~. The labels in the fluid.labelset~ correspond to the data points in the fluid.dataset~ by means of a shared identifier.

ul.li:
labelSet:
A fluid.labelset~ of labels for the source dataset

description.p: Given a fitted model, predict labels for a fluid.dataset~ and write these to a fluid.labelset~

ul.li:
dataSet:
A fluid.dataset~ of data points to predict labels for

labelSet:
A fluid.labelset~ to write the predicted labels into

description.p: Given a fitted model, predict a label for a data point in buffer~ and return to the caller

ul.li:
buffer:
A data point stored in a buffer~

description.p: The number of columns (dimensions) in this model or dataset / labeset
Resets the internal state of the model
The number of data points (entries / observations) in this model or dataset / labeset

method.name: load
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a dict. The message should take the form load dictionary <dictid>

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dump the state of this object from the dump outlet as a dict.

method.name: write
method.description: Save the internal state of the object to a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

method.name: read
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

description.p: The number of neighbours to consider
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
Whether the neighbours should be weighted by their distance so that closer points have more influence over determining the class. The default is 1 (true).
